human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel- and footwear-specific analytics behavior, style-color-size discipline, retailer and channel record awareness, compliance and disclosure boundary handling, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel, textiles, and footwear analytics using style-color-size records, retailer product-activity files, returns and inventory states, compliance evidence, and disclosure workbooks rather than generic retail BI work.

Authority sources cover named retailer operating guidance, GS1 apparel and general-merchandise references, FTC textile and care-label guidance, CPSC clothing and tracking-label controls, and IFRS and SEC disclosure discipline.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, denominator ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, terminology normalization, blocked prerequisites, adjacent-lane rejection, compliance handoff, tenant-specific adaptation, and sustainability-source conflict handling.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/apparel-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent merchandising, pricing, technical design, sourcing, compliance, and disclosure lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated apparel, textiles, and footwear analytics specialist for style-color-size performance analysis, inventory and returns reconciliation, compliance-aware reporting, and safe routing when authority shifts.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
